Explanation: This technical report presents a rigorous data integrity filtering workflow applied to chromatographic data, demonstrating competent data engineering practices. The methodology is clearly structured with sequential phases and explicit filtering criteria. However, several limitations reduce its overall quality. The visualization lacks critical axis labels and units, severely compromising quantitative interpretation of results. The symmetry ratio calculation method is problematic—it computes global symmetry across an unspecified dataset rather than per-peak metrics, limiting its reliability. The report acknowledges these limitations but doesn't provide statistical confidence intervals or quantitative validation of findings. While the writing is generally clear and the code is reproducible, the depth of analysis is limited; the study primarily validates data quality rather than providing substantive insights into peak morphology variations. The originality is moderate, as the approach applies standard filtering and normalization techniques without novel methodological contributions. Code inclusion is valuable for reproducibility but requires better documentation of the symmetry calculation logic.
